verses.1: The LORD says to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have held to subdue nations before him and strip kings of their armor, to open the doors before him, and the gates shall not be shut:
verses.3: I will give you the treasures of darkness
verses.5: I am the LORD, and there is no one else.
verses.6: that they may know from the rising of the sun,
verses.9: Woe to him who strives with his Maker—
verses.10: Woe to him who says to a father, ‘What have you become the father of?’
verses.11: The LORD, the Holy One of Israel
verses.12: I have made the earth, and created man on it.
verses.13: I have raised him up in righteousness,
verses.14: The LORD says: “The labor of Egypt,
verses.15: Most certainly you are a God who has hidden yourself,
verses.17: Israel will be saved by the LORD with an everlasting salvation.
verses.18: For the LORD who created the heavens,
verses.19: I have not spoken in secret,
verses.22: “Look to me, and be saved, all the ends of the earth;
verses.25: All the offspring of Israel will be justified in the LORD,
